figures.caption: Figure 4: Cross-reference resolution capability on Tier 3 queries. Tree-based approaches achieve 100% recall vs. 91.7% for Vector RAG.
Figure 5: Answer quality heatmap (Domain × Tier) for each retrieval method. Vector RAG shows weakness on medical T1 (0.50); Tree Reasoning achieves 1.00 on medical T2.
Table 5: FinanceBench performance by question reasoning type.

Figure 6: FinanceBench evaluation on real SEC filings. (A) Overall LLM-as-Judge quality scores: Tree Reasoning 0.938, Hybrid AHR 0.901, Vector RAG 0.821. (B) Performance by question type showing numerical reasoning as the most challenging category. (C) Latency vs. quality: Tree Reasoning shows higher and more consistent quality; Vector RAG is faster with higher variance.
Figure 1: (a) LLM-as-Judge quality score and (b) retrieval recall by query complexity tier. No single method dominates all tiers, supporting the case for adaptive retrieval.
Figure 2: Domain-wise performance comparison across financial, legal, and medical documents. Tree Reasoning dominates legal and medical domains; Vector RAG leads in financial.
Figure 3: Latency vs. answer quality trade-off. Vector RAG clusters in low-latency with high variance; Tree Reasoning shows higher but more consistent quality.
